'''''UFC Fight Night: Hunt vs. Bigfoot''''' (also known as '''''UFC Fight Night 33''''') was a [[mixed martial arts]] event held on December 7, 2013, at the [[Brisbane Entertainment Centre]] in [[Brisbane, Queensland|Brisbane]], Australia.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ufc.com/news/ufc-brisbane-hunt-vs-bigfoot-press-release-090913|title=Hunt vs. Bigfoot Headlines UFC in Brisbane|publisher=ufc.com|author=Staff|date=2013-09-09|access-date=2013-09-09}}</ref><ref name="FN33">{{cite web|url=http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/09/mark-hunt-vs-antonio-silva-targeted-for-ufcs-december-return-to-australia|title=Mark Hunt vs. Antonio Silva targeted for UFC's December return to Australia|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2013-09-04|access-date=2013-09-04|archive-date=4 September 2013|archive-url=https://archive.today/20130904221657/http://www.mmajunkie.com/news/2013/09/mark-hunt-vs-antonio-silva-targeted-for-ufcs-december-return-to-australia|url-status=dead}}</ref> The event was broadcast live on [[Fox Sports 1]].

==Background==
The event was the first the UFC has hosted in [[Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/boxing-mma/cagefighters-head-to-brisbane-for-ufc-event/story-fni2urrv-1226715009232|title=Cage-fighters head to Brisbane for UFC event |publisher=heraldsun.com.au|author=Chris Garry|date=2013-09-09|access-date=2013-09-09}}</ref> Overall, it was the fifth UFC event to be held in Australia.

The card was headlined by a heavyweight bout between [[Mark Hunt]] and [[Antônio Silva (fighter)|Antônio Silva]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theroar.com.au/2013/09/10/mark-hunt-to-headline-brisbane-ufc-card-in-december-interview/|title=Mark Hunt to headline Brisbane December UFC card |publisher=theroar.com.au|author=Jason Tuilo|date=2013-09-10|access-date=2013-09-10}}</ref> The main event ended in a rare majority draw decision; one judge scoring the fight 48–47 for Mark Hunt, while the other two saw it a 47–47 draw.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mmadecisions.com/decision/4738/Antonio-Silva-vs-Mark-Hunt|title=Antonio Silva drew with Mark Hunt|publisher=mmadecisions.com.|author=Staff|date=2013-12-07|access-date=2013-12-07|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217203442/http://www.mmadecisions.com/decision/4738/Antonio-Silva-vs-Mark-Hunt|archive-date=2013-12-17}}</ref>  Post-fight, UFC President Dana White said that the bout had earned ''Fight of the Night'' honors.{{fact|date=November 2021}}

Subsequently, Silva failed a post-fight test for elevated testosterone (he had been undergoing UFC-approved [[testosterone replacement therapy]]). Silva was suspended for nine months, retroactive to the date of the fight.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2013/12/antonio-bigfoot-silva-fails-post-fight-drug-test-stripped-of-50000-bonus/|title=Antonio Silva fails post-fight drug test|access-date=17 December 2013}}</ref> Due to Silva's positive test, Hunt was given the total of $100,000 from the ''Fight of the Night'' bonus award. For Hunt the bout result remains a "draw" on his official record, while in Silva's case it was overturned to a no-contest.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2013/12/antonio-bigfoot-silva-fails-post-fight-drug-test-stripped-of-50000-bonus/|title=Antonio Silva fails post-fight drug test|publisher=mmajunkie.com|access-date=17 December 2013}}</ref>

[[Brian Melancon]] was expected to face [[Robert Whittaker (fighter)|Robert Whittaker]] at the event. However, Melancon pulled out of the bout and subsequently announced his retirement due to [[renal]] stress. As a result, Whittaker was removed from the card as well.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2013/11/20/brian-melancon-injury-scraps-bout-with-robert-whittaker-from-ufc-fight-night-33-in-australia/|title=Brian Melancon injury scraps bout with Robert Whittaker from UFC Fight Night 33 in Australia |publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Matt Erickson|date=2013-11-20|access-date=2013-11-20}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ufc.com/news/Melancon-Retires-at-31|title=Melancon retires at 31 |publisher=ufc.com|author=Staff|date=2013-11-20|access-date=2013-11-20}}</ref>

[[Mitch Gagnon]] was expected to face [[Alex Caceres]] at the event. However, the bout was cancelled during the week leading up to the event due to an alleged [[visa (document)|visa]] issue for Gagnon, restricting his entry to Australia.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2013/12/alex-caceres-vs-mitch-gagnon-scratched-from-fridays-ufc-fight-night-33-main-card/|title=Alex Caceres vs. Mitch Gagnon scratched from Friday's UFC Fight Night 33 main card|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2013-12-02|access-date=2013-12-02|archive-date=2 December 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131202150942/http://mmajunkie.com/2013/12/alex-caceres-vs-mitch-gagnon-scratched-from-fridays-ufc-fight-night-33-main-card/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2013/12/ufc-fight-night-33-bonuses-rua-silva-and-hunt-pick-up-50000-awards/|title=UFC Fight Night 33 bonuses: Rua, Silva and Hunt pick up $500,000 awards|publisher=MMAjunkie.com|author=Staff|date=7 December 2013 |access-date=7 December 2013}}</ref> As Silva failed his post-fight drug test, his bonus share was taken back by the UFC and awarded to Hunt who received the full $170,000.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2013/12/antonio-bigfoot-silva-fails-post-fight-drug-test-stripped-of-50000-bonus|title=Antonio 'Bigfoot' Silva fails post-fight drug test, stripped of $170,000 bonus|publisher=MMAjunkie.com|author=John Morgan and Steven Marrocco|date=17 December 2013}}</ref>

* '''Fight of The Night: Mark Hunt vs. Antonio Silva'''
* '''Knockout of The Night: Maurício Rua'''
* '''Submission of the Night:'''  Not awarded as no matches ended by submission.
